Possible Cause: Not Applicable (N.A.)
Troubleshooting / Remedy: N.A.

Possible Cause: Temp. deviation between two sensors > limit
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Restart; if repeat, replace PT100 sensor.

Possible Cause: Chamber temp. > allowed limit during disinfection
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Restart and call service if repeated.

Possible Cause: Temp. difference between sensors > 3°C
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check chamber, boiler, and dryer PT100.

Possible Cause: Chamber temp. < allowed limit during disinfection
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Restart and call service if repeated.

Possible Cause: Washer spray arms are not turning
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check for blockages, spray arm bush, and cap.

Possible Cause: RO-water conductivity > set point value
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Ensure DI-water quality; check sensor.

Possible Cause: Temp. reached 98°C during holding time
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check chamber PT100 and heating contactor.

Possible Cause: Non-existing valve used in program
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Call service engineer.

Possible Cause: Disinf. temp. not reached set value (>70°C)
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check chamber PT100 and heating contactors.

Possible Cause: Wash chamber temp. exceeded safety limit
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Restart machine; check heating components.

Possible Cause: Rinse water conductivity outside tolerance
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check main water supply and sensor.

Possible Cause: Door end switches (Clean Side) not recognized
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check/adjust door end switch or mechanics.

Possible Cause: User activated "Stop" key (interruption)
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Restart the program.

Possible Cause: Incorrect rack/insert basket used for program
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Use proper rack and restart program.

Possible Cause: Last program step not set to "Program end"
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Verify program steps (must end with type 29).

Possible Cause: First program step not set to "Preparation"
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Verify program steps (must start with type 28).

Possible Cause: Final-level reached before basic-level
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Reset unit and restart program.

Possible Cause: Clean Side door activity timed out
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check door mechanics for jammed objects.

Possible Cause: Step holding temp. is lower than dosing temp.
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Verify program step temperature settings.

Possible Cause: Dryer failed to reach target temp. within timeout
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check heating elements, contactor, and dryer.

Possible Cause: Non-existing dosing unit number selected
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Verify program dosing step parameters.

Possible Cause: Program accessing boiler water without preparation
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Verify program steps.

Possible Cause: Required water volume reached too soon
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check level/pressure sensors and water valve.

Possible Cause: Maximum dosing time exceeded
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check dosing pump/sensor; recalibrate system.

Possible Cause: Safety panel of Clean Side door was pressed
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Remove obstructing object.

Possible Cause: Boiler contactor monitoring reacted
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Test and exchange contactor if necessary.

Possible Cause: Maximum water volume exceeded
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check level/pressure sensors and water valve.

Possible Cause: Too much water in boiler; valve or switch defect
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Activate drain valves; check level sensors.

Possible Cause: Door end switches (Unclean Side) not recognized
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check/adjust door end switches.

Possible Cause: Requested water volume not reached (timeout)
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check water supply, valves, and drain leaks.

Possible Cause: Wash pressure control detected low pressure
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check pump, pressure sensor, and foaming.

Possible Cause: Unclean Side door activity timed out
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check door mechanics for jammed objects.

Possible Cause: Rest-filling after pump start failed
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check water availability and filling system.

Possible Cause: Chamber not emptied within time limit
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check drain pump and level sensors.

Possible Cause: Wash pressure lost due to foaming or insertion
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check endoscope insertion and foaming.

Possible Cause: Too much water in wash chamber
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check inlet valve and level system.

Possible Cause: Door detected open during wash cycle
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check door micro switch and mechanics.

Possible Cause: Conveyor emergency stop activated
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check emergency switch and safety hood.

Possible Cause: Safety bar activated by object or operator
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check for obstructions or safety bar defect.

Possible Cause: Inadmissible value or pressure drop in test sensors
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check sensor connections; run Pressure Test (P15).

Possible Cause: Inadmissible value / interruption at tank sensor
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check sensor and connections.

Possible Cause: Clean Side door detected open during program
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check door open sensor.

Possible Cause: Unclean Side door detected open during program
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check door open sensor.

Possible Cause: Cleaner container empty or sensor defect
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Refill container.

Possible Cause: Dosing unit not working after activation
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Run P16 (Rinsing dosage system).

Possible Cause: Dosing unit tolerance, flow, or activation errors
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check impulse sensors, pumps, and valves; run P16.

Possible Cause: Chamber not emptied within time limit
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check drain pump and level recognition.

Possible Cause: Dosage system calibration lost or missing
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Perform new calibration.

Possible Cause: Chamber failed to reach target temperature
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check heating elements, contactors, and sensors.

Possible Cause: Circulation pump motor overcharged or phase failed
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Reset motor protection switch; check for shorts.

Possible Cause: Temperature exceeded 60°C (Endoscope programs)
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check wash program parameters.

Possible Cause: Service counter reached pre-set number of cycles
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Carry out maintenance and reset counter.

Possible Cause: Signal loss from boiler normal level float switch
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check float switch and boiler drain valve.

Possible Cause: Boiler failed to fill within time limit
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check inlet valve and float switch.

Possible Cause: Boiler failed to reach set temperature
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check heating elements and thermostat.

Possible Cause: Interruption at boiler temperature sensor
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check/exchange PT100 sensor.

Possible Cause: Short circuit at boiler temperature sensor
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check/exchange PT100 sensor.

Possible Cause: Recirculation pump motor overcharged
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check connections, balance, and drain valve.

Possible Cause: Maintenance interval for filter reached
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Replace sterile filter.

Possible Cause: Communication failure in interbus system
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check connections on PLC and touchscreen.

Possible Cause: Fault in connected devices or Fuse F135
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check fuse and search for short circuits.

Possible Cause: Communication failure with scanner unit
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Restart machine; check scanner connections.

Possible Cause: Communication failure with printer unit
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check power supply and printer cable.

Possible Cause: Communication failure with external IT system
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Check machine-to-site IT connections.

Possible Cause: Batch counter reached maintenance limit
Troubleshooting / Remedy: Complete service maintenance and reset counter
